Who We Are

The Devastating Delta

I

Academics

Excellence in the classroom and beyond.

II

Brotherhood

Bonds that last long after graduation.

III

Culture

Pride in Latino heritage and identity.

IV

Service

Giving back to the communities that shaped us.

Our History

From Cornell
to Philadelphia

February 19, 1982

La Hermandad Founded

13 visionaries establish Lambda Upsilon Lambda at Cornell University — the only Latino fraternity to charter at all eight Ivy League schools.

April 24, 1988

Devastating Delta Chartered

Delta becomes the third chapter in LUL history, established across Penn, Drexel, Temple, and Haverford — one of the most expansive chapters in the fraternity.

1991

Delta's Influence Spreads

1996

Building the Infrastructure

Delta hermanos help unify Penn's Latinx student groups into the Latinx Coalition — and co-found Onda Latina, Penn's first Latin dance troupe — laying institutional groundwork that still stands today.

1999

La Casa Latina Opens

Penn's Center for Hispanic Excellence — La Casa Latina — is inaugurated, with Delta among the organizations that helped build the case for it.

Annually

Pa'Lante Banquet

Delta's flagship event, started to give hermanos who couldn't travel home for the holidays a place to belong. Now in its 34th year — proceeds fund the chapter's Brown and Gold Christmas Initiative.

Today

The Standard Continues

Flagship Event

34th Annual Pa'Lante Banquet

The chapter's signature night — performances, remarks, and a packed room of hermanos, alumni, and friends. Proceeds go to the Brown and Gold Christmas Initiative.

Hall of Flags · Philadelphia, PA
+ Add to Calendar
Flagship Event
November 15, 2026

The 34th Annual Pa'Lante Banquet

Pa'Lante began as a place for hermanos who couldn't make it home for the holidays. Now in its 34th year, it's Delta's defining night — performances, remarks, and a packed room of brothers, alumni, and friends. Proceeds fund the Brown and Gold Christmas Initiative.

Date
November 15, 2026
Venue
Hall of Flags · Philadelphia, PA
Recognition · 2025–26

Awards & Accolades

I
Chapter Award

Silver Accreditation Award

Earned for meeting the national fraternity's highest standards of chapter excellence in programming, brotherhood, and operations.

Devastating Delta · 2025–26
II
Individual Award

Mosaic Leader Award

Presented to a brother who exemplifies leadership across diverse communities and builds bridges beyond the chapter.

Jesus Vaca-Garcia "Ace" · Spring '25
III
Individual Award

Fraternity Member of the Year

Given for exceptional dedication to the chapter and the broader Penn community. William Ayala set that standard on and off campus.

William "Ace" Ayala · Fall '24
IV
Chapter Award

Professional Development Program of the Year

Recognized alongside Sigma Lambda Upsilon, Señoritas Latinas Unidas, for standout career programming across Greek life.

Delta × SLU · 2025–26
V
Chapter Award

Wellness Program of the Year

Awarded for our health and wellness collaboration with Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc. — building community around well-being.

Delta × ΔΣΘ · 2025–26
Fall 2025 – Spring 2026

Delta Network

Alumni Directory

The Delta network spans law, medicine, finance, education, and public service across Philadelphia and beyond. Keeping it current is how the chapter delivers real value to the brothers coming up behind it.

Update Your Information

Keep the Directory Current

Industry, city, and the best way to reach you. Five minutes, and it's the difference between an undergrad finding a mentor or not.

Update My Information →

Or email lul.devastatingdelta@gmail.com directly.

For Undergrads

Interested in
Becoming a Hermano?

If you're a student at Penn, Drexel, Temple, or Haverford and you feel called to something bigger — here's how it starts.

1

Learn Who We Are

Read up on La Unidad Latina, our four pillars, and what Delta Chapter has meant to Philadelphia since 1988. Start at launidadlatina.org.

2

Follow & Show Up Online

Follow @lul_devastatingdelta on Instagram. Engage. Let us see who you are before you reach out — presence matters.

3

Submit Your Interest Form

4

Come to an Interest Meeting

We host interest meetings every semester. No commitment — just come, meet the chapter and start building genuine connections.

Fill Out Interest Form → Save the Dates & Sign Up
Prospective Hermano

Get on Our Radar

Tell us a bit about you. A current hermano will reach out — and you'll get our upcoming dates straight to your calendar.

Submitting opens a pre-filled email to the chapter. Nothing is sent automatically.

Chapter Publications

Delta Newsletter

The chapter newsletter lives here once it's ready. To add an issue: upload the PDF to Google Drive, copy the shareable link, and paste it into one of the cards below.

Vol. 1 · Coming Soon

Fall 2026 Newsletter

First issue of the Devastating Delta newsletter. Upload the PDF to Google Drive and replace the link below when ready.

Read Issue →
DRAFT
+
Add Next Issue

Copy a card from above, paste it here, and update the link & issue number.

How to add a newsletter issue
  1. Finish the newsletter (Google Docs, Canva, whatever you use) and export as PDF.
  2. Upload the PDF to Google Drive → right-click → Share → set to "Anyone with the link can view" → copy link.
  3. Open this HTML file in a text editor, search for YOUR_NEWSLETTER_LINK_VOL1, and paste your Drive link there.
  4. Save and re-upload the file to GitHub. Done.
Keep the Network Strong

Update Your Directory

Industry, city, LinkedIn, best way to reach you. Five minutes keeps the whole Delta network connected — for the undergrads coming up and the alumni who came before.

Submissions go directly to chapter leadership. Directory stays internal.
